title: The schema therapy clinician_s guide _ a complete resource

node_types.description: An individual receiving schema therapy, with associated clinical, demographic, and treatment information.

properties.name: motivation_level
properties.description: Assessment of patient's motivation and commitment

properties.name: inclusion_criteria_met
properties.description: Whether patient meets inclusion criteria for schema therapy

properties.name: exclusion_criteria_met
properties.description: Whether patient meets exclusion criteria for schema therapy

properties.description: Roles held by the therapist (e.g., lead, co-therapist)

properties.name: supervision_status
properties.description: Current supervision or peer supervision status

node_types.description: A psychological state or mode as defined in schema therapy (e.g., Vulnerable Child, Punitive Parent).

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category of the mode (e.g., Child, Parent, Coping, Healthy)

properties.name: associated_needs
properties.description: Core needs associated with the mode

supported_by_chapters: 3 The Integrated Schema Therapy Treatment Program

node_types.description: A core maladaptive belief or schema developed in childhood, grouped by content area.

properties.name: schema_name
properties.description: Name of the early maladaptive schema

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Etiological factors (e.g., unmet needs, temperament, environment)

node_types.description: A fundamental psychological need relevant to schema therapy (e.g., secure attachment, autonomy).
A therapeutic intervention or technique used in schema therapy.

properties.name: target_modes
properties.description: Schema modes targeted by the intervention

node_types.description: A therapy session (individual or group) within the schema therapy program.

properties.name: session_format
properties.description: Format (e.g., Welcome, Education, Mode Awareness, Mode Management, Experiential Mode Work)

node_types.description: A tool or instrument used to assess schemas, modes, or therapy progress (e.g., Schema Mode Inventory).

edge_types.description: Indicates that a patient exhibits or is currently in a specific schema mode.

properties.description: How often the mode is activated

edge_types.description: Indicates that a patient has a particular early maladaptive schema.

properties.name: endorsement_level
properties.description: Degree to which the schema is present

edge_types.description: Links a schema mode to the core psychological need(s) it represents or is associated with.
Indicates that an early maladaptive schema arises from unmet core needs.
Indicates that a specific intervention is designed to address or modify a schema mode.
Indicates that a patient has received or is receiving a specific intervention.
